attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: It loves sweet nectar. To keep all the nectar to itself, it hurls rivals away with its prized horn.
To shelter itself from cold, wintry winds, it covers itself with a cloak made of twigs and leaves.
It loves the honey of flowers and steals honey collected by Combee.
At night, Combee sleep in a group of about a hundred, packed closely together in a lump.

attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: It houses its colony in cells in its body and releases various pheromones to make those grubs do its bidding.
It nimbly dashes about to avoid getting pecked by bird Pokémon that would love to make off with its small, nutrient-rich storage ball.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: As a bud, it barely moves. It sits still, placidly waiting for sunlight to appear.

attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: After absorbing plenty of sunlight, Cherrim takes this form. It’s full of energy while it’s like this, and its liveliness will go on until sundown.

abilities.text: If you have Arceus or Arceus ex in play, attacks used by this Pokémon do +30 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It attracts prey with its sweet-smelling saliva, then chomps down. It takes a whole day to eat prey.

abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may take a {G} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your {G} Pokémon.

flavorText: It is smart enough to hunt in packs. It uses a variety of cries for communicating with others.

attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: If you are burned by the flames it shoots from its mouth, the pain will never go away.

abilities.text: If you have Arceus or Arceus ex in play, this Pokémon has no Retreat Cost.

attacks.text: If this Pokémon has damage on it, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: It dwells in volcanic caves. It digs in with its cross-shaped feet to crawl on ceilings and walls.
The fur on its body naturally repels water. It can stay dry even when it plays in the water.
Its long ears are superb sensors. It can distinguish the movements of things in water and tell what they are.
Its two whiskers provide a sensitive radar. Even in muddy waters, it can detect its prey’s location.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 more damage. If tails, this Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: It is extremely protective of its territory. If any foe approaches, it attacks using vicious tremors.
It is said that several Snorunt gather under giant leaves and live together in harmony.

attacks.text: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: When it finds humans or Pokémon it likes, it freezes them and takes them to its chilly den, where they become decorations.
During cold seasons, it migrates to the mountain’s lower reaches. It returns to the snow-covered summit in the spring.

abilities.text: If you have Arceus or Arceus ex in play, attacks used by this Pokémon cost 1 less {C} Energy.

flavorText: It lives a quiet life on mountains that are perpetually covered in snow. It hides itself by whipping up blizzards.

abilities.text: During Pokémon Checkup, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, do 10 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: It soars across the sky in a form that greatly resembles the creator of all things. Perhaps this imitation of appearance is Palkia’s strategy for gaining Arceus’s powers.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: When the water warms, they inflate the flotation sac on their heads and drift languidly on the sea in packs.

attacks.text: This attack also does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: When it is angered, it immediately discharges the energy stored in the pouches in its cheeks.

abilities.text: If you have Arceus or Arceus ex in play, this Pokémon takes −30 damage from attacks.

attacks.text: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Its tail discharges electricity into the ground, protecting it from getting shocked.
It stores static electricity in its fur for discharging. It gives off sparks if a storm approaches.

attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to use an attack, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn't happen.

flavorText: It stimulates its own muscles with electricity, so it can move quickly. It eases its soreness with electricity, too, so it can recover quickly as well.
It is said that happiness will come to those who see a gathering of Clefairy dancing under a full moon.

attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon do −30 damage.

flavorText: A timid fairy Pokémon that is rarely seen, it will run and hide the moment it senses people.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent reveals a random card from their hand and shuffles it into their deck.

flavorText: It wraps its opponent in its gas-like body, slowly weakening its prey by poisoning it through the skin.
It likes to lurk in the dark and tap shoulders with a gaseous hand. Its touch causes endless shuddering.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: To steal the life of its target, it slips into the prey’s shadow and silently waits for an opportunity.

abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may choose either player. Look at the top card of that player's deck.

flavorText: Its flat, thin body is always stuck on walls. Its shape appears to have some meaning.

abilities.text: If you have Arceus or Arceus ex in play, this Pokémon has no Retreat Cost.

attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Its electricity-like body can enter some kinds of machines and take control in order to make mischief.

attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: Although it always pretends to be a tree, its composition appears more similar to rock than to vegetation.
This Pokémon lives and nests on a riverbank. After playing in the mud, it won’t be able to settle down unless it washes its body.

attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Rolling Spin attack does +60 damage.

flavorText: Donphan is covered in tough hide, so even being hit by a car won’t faze this Pokémon. However, it is extremely susceptible to rain.
Born deep underground, this Pokémon becomes a pupa after eating enough dirt to make a mountain.
Its shell is as hard as bedrock, and it is also very strong. Its thrashing can topple a mountain.

abilities.text: If you have Arceus or Arceus ex in play, attacks used by this Pokémon do +30 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Extremely strong, it can change the landscape. It is so insolent that it doesn’t care about others.
It moves less than an inch a year, but when it’s in a jam, it will spin and drill down into the ground in a split second.
It never skips its daily yoga training. It heightens its inner strength through meditation.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Through yoga training, it gained the psychic power to predict its foe’s next move.
It skulks in caves, and when prey or an enemy passes by, it leaps out and chomps them. The force of its attack sometimes chips its teeth.
In rare cases, it molts and sheds its scales. Medicine containing its scales as an ingredient will make a weary body feel invigorated.

attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: It emits ultrasonic waves from its mouth to check its surroundings. Even in tight caves, Zubat flies around with skill.
It loves to drink other creatures’ blood. It’s said that if it finds others of its kind going hungry, it sometimes shares the blood it’s gathered.

abilities.text: Once during your turn, if you have Arceus or Arceus ex in play, you may do 30 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Both of its legs have turned into wings. Without a sound, Crobat flies swiftly toward its prey and sinks its fangs into the nape of its target’s neck.
Inflating its poison sacs, it fills the area with an odd sound and hits flinching opponents with a poison jab.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Do 20 damage to this Pokémon instead of the usual amount for this Special Condition.

flavorText: Swaying and dodging the attacks of its foes, it weaves its flexible body in close, then lunges out with its poisonous claws.
The electromagnetic waves emitted by the units at the sides of its head expel antigravity, which allows it to float.
Three Magnemite are linked by a strong magnetic force. Earaches will occur if you get too close.

abilities.text: If you have Arceus or Arceus ex in play, this Pokémon takes −30 damage from attacks.

flavorText: As it zooms through the sky, this Pokémon seems to be receiving signals of unknown origin while transmitting signals of unknown purpose.

attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a {M} Pokémon, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: It uses its docile-looking face to lull foes into complacency, then bites with its huge, relentless jaws.

attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.

flavorText: Ancient people believed that the pattern on Bronzor’s back contained a mysterious power.

attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: In ages past, this Pokémon was revered as a bringer of rain. It was found buried in the ground.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, during your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: Radiant light caused Dialga to take on a form bearing a striking resemblance to the creator Pokémon. Dialga now wields such colossal strength that one must conclude this is its true form.

attacks.text: Discard 2 random Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon is said to live in a world on the reverse side of ours, where common knowledge is distorted and strange.
Its ability to evolve into many forms allows it to adapt smoothly and perfectly to any environment.

attacks.text: This Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It is not satisfied unless it eats over 880 pounds of food every day. When it is done eating, it goes promptly to sleep.
It always stands on one foot. It changes feet so fast, the movement can rarely be seen.

attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand.

flavorText: Its eyes are specially developed to enable it to see clearly even in murky darkness and minimal light.
They flock around mountains and fields, chasing after bug Pokémon. Their singing is noisy and annoying.
Recognizing their own weakness, they always live in a group. When alone, a Staravia cries noisily.

attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: When Staravia evolve into Staraptor, they leave the flock to live alone. They have sturdy wings.

abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, your Active Basic Pokemon's Retreat Cost is 1 less.

flavorText: It can dissolve toxins in the air to instantly transform ruined land into a lush field of flowers.

attacks.text: Discard a random Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: According to the legends of Sinnoh, this Pokémon emerged from an egg and shaped all there is in this world.

abilities.text: This Pokémon can't be affected by any Special Conditions.

attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.

trainer.text: Heal 40 damage from each of your Pokémon that has any {W} Energy attached.
Put 1 random Basic Pokémon from your discard pile into your hand.
During this turn, attacks used by your Snorlax, Heracross, and Staraptor cost 2 less {C} Energy.
During your opponent's next turn, all of your {M} Pokémon take −20 damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

The fur on its body naturally repels water. It can stay dry even when it plays in the water.
